sector_rotation.explanation: Cyclicals are outperforming defensives, suggesting investors are comfortable with risk. Typically seen in early/mid expansion phases.

news_headlines.title: 2026 Porsche Cayenne Coupe Electric Is Here Because 40% Of Cayenne Buyers Go For The Coupe - Jalopnik
news_headlines.description: It's launching with the same base, S and 1,139-horsepower Turbo trims as the regular Cayenne Electric, with prices that start at around $5,000 more.

news_headlines.title: Futures: Two AI Plays Jump After Iran News Hits Market - Investor's Business Daily
news_headlines.description: ServiceNow, Freeport-McMoRan and Tesla also weighed on stocks Thursday.

news_headlines.title: Behind Cursor’s Deal With SpaceX, Anthropic and Compute Costs Loomed Large - The Information
news_headlines.description: Weeks before Cursor agreed to a $60 billion potential takeover from SpaceX, the hot AI coding startup set out to raise billions of dollars. As its leaders made the rounds among tech’s largest investors, they started to hear an unfamiliar message: No, thanks. …

news_headlines.title: NTSB report describes chaos at LaGuardia Airport ahead of deadly Air Canada crash - Gothamist
news_headlines.description: The March 22 crash came as seven different emergency vehicles scrambled to respond to another plane at the airport.

news_headlines.title: Japan core inflation accelerates after five months as Iran war stokes energy worries - CNBC
news_headlines.description: Government data showed the inflation figure — which strips out prices of fresh food — was in line with the 1.8% expected by economists polled by Reuters.

news_headlines.title: Why Intel’s stock is on track for a historic surge after earnings - MarketWatch
news_headlines.description: Intel shares could see their best post-earnings performance on record and finally surpass their 2000 closing high amid a ‘CPU renaissance’

news_headlines.title: Trump Says the US Is Weighing Purchase of Spirit Airlines - Bloomberg.com
news_headlines.description: President Donald Trump said he is considering having the US purchase Spirit Aviation Holdings Inc., saying it could be a potentially good investment for the federal government.

news_headlines.title: French police probe suspected weather device tampering after odd Polymarket bet - NPR
news_headlines.description: The incident is the latest eyebrow-raising bet on Polymarket, as allegations of rigging and manipulation continue to haunt the popular prediction market site.

news_headlines.title: Nike Cuts 1,400 Jobs in Operations and Tech in Latest Round of Layoffs - WWD
news_headlines.description: Nike cut approximately 1,400 jobs in operations and technology across North America, Europe and Asia on Thursday in latest round of layoffs.

news_headlines.title: Trump says Americans should expect higher gas prices for 'a little while' - CNBC
news_headlines.description: Trump's remarks came as most Americans say they have cut spending due to high gas prices, according to the latest CNBC All-America Economic survey.

news_headlines.title: Chip Stocks On Epic Run, Fueled By AI Megatrend, Analog Recovery - Investor's Business Daily
news_headlines.description: The Philadelphia semiconductor index, known as SOX, is on a historic run, fueled by chip stocks hitting new highs.

news_headlines.title: Air travel prices likely to get worse in coming weeks, Chevron CEO says - CBS News
news_headlines.description: Travelers could see airline fares rise and fewer flights available in the coming weeks, Chevron CEO Mike Wirth said in an interview with "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan."

news_headlines.title: Tesla stock dives on news that it earned next to nothing on cars in Q1, and plans to spend $25 billion in CapEx anyway - Fortune
news_headlines.description: Almost all of Tesla's Q1 earnings flowed from the carbon credits line and gains on trimming its Bitcoin trove.

news_headlines.title: ServiceNow Stock Has Worst Day Ever on Disappointing Sales - Yahoo Finance
news_headlines.description: (Bloomberg) -- ServiceNow Inc. shares plunged the most ever after the provider of software for business tasks reported results that disappointed investors...

news_headlines.title: The FDA gives the green light to the first gene therapy for deafness - NPR
news_headlines.description: The treatment, developed by Regeneron Pharmaceuticals, is for a very rare form of deafness. But it represents a medical milestone.

nyt_headlines.title: In Wine Country, Sales Are Down and Fraud is Rampant
nyt_headlines.abstract: The industry’s murky supply chain has long attracted scammers and con artists. In the words of one expert, “Wine and fraud go hand in hand.”

nyt_headlines.title: Iran’s Meme War Against Trump Ushers In a Future of ‘Slopaganda’
nyt_headlines.abstract: The country’s propaganda apparatus has harnessed artificial intelligence tools to reach a global online audience primed for irony, irreverence and trolling.

nyt_headlines.title: More Used Electric Cars Are Coming at More Affordable Prices
nyt_headlines.abstract: The leases on hundreds of thousands of battery-powered cars and trucks will end in the next three years, and many will end up on used-car lots.

nyt_headlines.title: If On Shoes Are for Everyone, Are They Still Ons?
nyt_headlines.abstract: The company finds itself at a critical juncture that others have botched: How does it stay a brand for serious athletes while keeping up its breakneck growth?

nyt_headlines.title: SiriusXM Said to Be in Early Talks to Acquire iHeartMedia
nyt_headlines.abstract: A deal, which was described by two people familiar with the discussions, would unite two major providers of traditional and satellite radio.

nyt_headlines.title: 31 Sloths Acquired by an Orlando Animal Attraction Died, Officials Say
nyt_headlines.abstract: Many of the deaths were attributed to a lack of heat at an unauthorized warehouse that Sloth World was using, officials said.

nyt_headlines.title: The ‘Lasting Damage’ of Pirro’s Abandoned Fed Investigation
nyt_headlines.abstract: The Trump administration’s attacks on the Federal Reserve have rattled confidence in the central bank’s ability to operate independently before a leadership transition.

nyt_headlines.title: Over 200,000 Heated Socks Sold at Costco Recalled After Reports of Burns
nyt_headlines.abstract: The socks, from the brand 32 Degrees, were linked to reports of first- and second-degree burns, according to U.S. product safety regulators.
Robots assemble vehicles at Geely’s Qiantang manufacturing plant in Hangzhou, last year,

nyt_headlines.title: Paramount Throws Party for Trump as It Awaits Approval of Warner Bros. Deal
nyt_headlines.abstract: The gathering included executives and leading journalists from CBS News, which Paramount owns, and the head of the Justice Department, which is reviewing the acquisition.

nyt_headlines.title: Judge Imposes Sanctions on Woman and Her Lawyer in Suit Against Leon Black
nyt_headlines.abstract: A federal judge said the lawyer representing an unnamed plaintiff who accused the billionaire investor of abusing her had “lied repeatedly to the court.”

nyt_headlines.title: Justice Department Drops Criminal Investigation Into the Fed
nyt_headlines.abstract: The decision to end the inquiry into Jerome H. Powell’s handling of the Federal Reserve’s renovation could allow Kevin M. Warsh, the president’s pick for Fed chair, to be confirmed.

nyt_headlines.title: How Kevin Warsh Could Shrink the Fed’s Footprint in Financial Markets
nyt_headlines.abstract: President Trump’s nominee to become the next chair of the Federal Reserve wants to overhaul the central bank, including its more than $6 trillion balance sheet.

nyt_headlines.title: Tariffs Raised Consumers’ Prices, but the Refunds Go Only to Businesses
nyt_headlines.abstract: Many families felt the sting of the president’s now-illegal tariffs, but companies have said little about whether they will share the $166 billion coming back to them.

nyt_headlines.title: Justice Dept. Closes Criminal Investigation of Fed Chair
nyt_headlines.abstract: Also, the U.S. is sending top envoys to Pakistan for Iran peace talks. Here’s the latest at the end of Friday.
